headline: Morgan Stanley Ups Caterpillar (CAT) Target to $430, Flags Macro Risks to Earnings
summary: Caterpillar Inc. (NYSE:CAT) is included among the 10 Best US Stocks to Invest in for Long Term. On April 15, Morgan Stanley raised its price recommendation on Caterpillar Inc. (NYSE:CAT) to $430 from $425. It kept an Underweight rating on the shares. The firm said that within machinery and construction, it prefers “idiosyncratic stories where […]

headline: 3 Reasons to Avoid CAT and 1 Stock to Buy Instead
summary: What a time it’s been for Caterpillar. In the past six months alone, the company’s stock price has increased by a massive 55.7%, setting a new 52-week high of $810.23 per share. This was partly due to its solid quarterly results, and the run-up might have investors contemplating their next move.

headline: S&P 500 Stocks: Equipment Giant Flashes Buy Signal On Earnings
summary: United Rentals is leading all S&P 500 stocks early Thursday on the strength of Q1 results and an updated earnings outlook delivered after Wednesday's close.  United Rentals reported its first profit gain and its strongest revenue growth since the fourth quarter of 2024.  "The increases to our full-year guidance are supported by the momentum we are carrying into our busy season and the growth opportunities our customers see on the horizon, particularly within large projects and key verticals," CEO Matthew Flannery said in the earnings release.

headline: Is It Too Late To Consider Caterpillar (CAT) After Its Strong Share Price Rally?
summary: Investors may be asking whether Caterpillar at around US$808.87 still offers value after a strong run, or if they are arriving late to the story. The stock has recent returns of 5.0% over 7 days, 15.3% over 30 days, 35.2% year to date and 176.4% over 1 year, which puts price action front and center in any assessment of risk and reward. Recent coverage has focused on Caterpillar as a key name in capital goods and heavy machinery, with attention on how investors are treating companies tied to...
